element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core combat mechanics introduced in opening stage
Alternative damage method via aerial collision
Resource gathering for score and life extension
10 per stage, substantial score increases, encourages thoroughness
Time-limited bonus collection feeding wheel-spin rewards
Risk-free reward system: extra lives, health, points
Life system core; depletable via enemy contact or fall
Escape sequence: vertical traversal avoiding lava waves
Jasmine ride: unrestricted movement, timed to 'A Whole New World' melody
Stages 1, 6, 7 contain bosses; Stage 7 is final confrontation
Environmental threats including lava, spikes, falling mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Enemy defeat via apple throw or jump
typical_tension_triggers: Lava wave chase in Cave of Wonders (Stage 4)
Scarab appearance (time pressure: collect before disappearance)
Approaching stage end with low health
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating safe exploration/collection phases (Stages 2-5 reach-the-end) with spike encounters (boss stages 1, 6, 7); mid-game peak tension at lava escape; final stage sustains highest difficulty

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Red gems (10 per stage) placed off main path; scarab chests encourage thorough exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate: Stage 1 introduces boss, basic enemies, and core mechanics simultaneously; Nintendo Power noted game 'not as challenging as expected from Capcom'
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Peak tension: Stage 4 (Cave of Wonders) features self-scrolling lava chase with vertical hazard avoidance; Stage 5 transitions to free-flying bonus
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high difficulty: Stages 6-7 contain boss encounters; Stage 7 final boss represents climax; health management critical
notable_spikes: Stage 1: Boss encounter immediately teaches pattern recognition
Stage 4: Lava escape sequence forces skill mastery (outrunning procedural hazards)
Stage 6-7: Boss gauntlet with minimal recovery windows

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages 2, 3, 5: Linear horizontal advancement requiring enemy avoidance, gem collection, scarab pursuit. No boss gate; completion = stage end reaching.

Stage 4 (Cave of Wonders): Self-scrolling Magic Carpet forced through vertical obstacle field while lava waves pursue from below. Asymmetrical tension: player must navigate up/down corridor without being trapped.
Between Stages 5-6: Free-flying Magic Carpet with Jasmine. Omnidirectional movement, no threat, pure collection. Timed to musical phrase. Serves as relief and score padding.
Stages 1, 6, 7: Terminal stage goal is boss defeat rather than end-reaching. Requires pattern learning and sustained combat. Stage 7 caps narrative arc.

sources_used: Wikipedia - Disney's Aladdin (SNES video game) [gameplay, stage count, boss structure, magic carpet mechanics]
The Cutting Room Floor - Disney's Aladdin (SNES) [developer attribution, level select, unused enemies context]
